zh.Bronkhorst, Steenderen, Gelderland, Neth: 荷蘭蓋爾德藍德斯廷德倫布洛霍斯特
zh.Bronckhorst, Gelderland, Nederland: 荷蘭蓋爾德藍德布洛可斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Willem I van Bronchorst於1154年出生在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德斯廷德倫布洛霍斯特時，他的父親Gijsbert I van Bronckhorst62歲，母親Heilwige van Roode37歲。他和Gertrud von Ahaus至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1226年1月26日在他的家鄉享年72歲。

zh-hans.Bronkhorst, Steenderen, Gelderland, Neth: 荷兰蓋爾德藍德斯廷德倫布洛霍斯特
zh-hans.Bronckhorst, Gelderland, Nederland: 荷兰蓋爾德藍德布洛可斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Willem I van Bronchorst于1154年出生在荷兰蓋爾德藍德斯廷德倫布洛霍斯特时，他的父亲Gijsbert I van Bronckhorst62岁，母亲Heilwige van Roode37岁。他和Gertrud von Ahaus至少有4个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1226年1月26日在他的家乡享年72岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Bronchorst
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: habitational name from a small town called Bronkhorst by the river Ijssel near Steenderen in Gelderland province. The placename derives from bronk see Bronk + horst ‘higher located brushwood’.

variants.name: Brunkhorst
meanings: North German: habitational name from a place called Brunkhorst, a cognate of the Dutch name   Bronkhorst  .

variants.name: Bronk
meanings: Dutch: nickname for someone known for sulking or flaunting behavior, from bronken (brunken, perhaps also pronken) ‘to sulk, to strut, to flaunt’.
Dutch: from the personal name Bronne, a variant of Bruno + the diminutive suffix -ke.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from the village of Bronki, in eastern Poland.

nameMeanings.name: Willem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Willem I van Bronchorst was born in 1154, in Bronkhorst, Steenderen, Gelderland, Netherlands, his father, Gijsbert I van Bronckhorst, was 62 and his mother, Heilwige van Roode, was 37. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Gertrud von Ahaus. He died on 26 January 1226, in his hometown, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Willem I van Bronchorst est né en 1154, à Bronkhorst, Steenderen, Gueldre, Pays-Bas, son père, Gijsbert I van Bronckhorst, avait 62 ans et sa mère, Heilwige van Roode, avait 37 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Gertrud von Ahaus. Il est décédé le 26 janvier 1226, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 72 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Willem I van Bronchorst 1154 in Bronkhorst, Steenderen, Geldern, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gijsbert I van Bronckhorst 62 und seine Mutter Heilwige van Roode 37. Er hatte mit Gertrud von Ahaus mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 26. Januar 1226 im Alter von 72 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Willem I van Bronchorst è nato il nel 1154 nel Bronkhorst, Steenderen, Gelderland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Gijsbert I van Bronckhorst, aveva 62 anni e il suo madre, Heilwige van Roode, aveva 37 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Gertrud von Ahaus. Morì il 26 gennaio 1226, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 72 anni.

ja.Bronkhorst, Steenderen, Gelderland, Neth: オランダジェルダーランドスティーンデレンブロンクhオルスト
ja.Bronckhorst, Gelderland, Nederland: オランダジェルダーランドブロンックhオルスト
ja.lifeSummary: Willem I van Bronchorstが1154年にオランダジェルダーランドスティーンデレンブロンクhオルストで生まれた時，父Gijsbert I van Bronckhorstは62歳，母Heilwige van Roodeは37歳であった。彼はGertrud von Ahausとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1226年1月26日に故郷で72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Willem I van Bronchorst(이)가 1154년 네덜란드 글델랜드 스티더 브론k호르스트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gijsbert I van Bronckhorst(은)는 62세였고, 그의 어머니 Heilwige van Roode(은)는 37세였다. 그는 Gertrud von Ahaus(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1226년 1월 26일 그의 고향에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Willem I van Bronchorst nasceu em 1154, em Bronkhorst, Steenderen, Gheldria, Países Baixos, seu pai, Gijsbert I van Bronckhorst, tinha 62 anos e sua mãe, Heilwige van Roode, tinha 37 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha com Gertrud von Ahaus. Ele faleceu em 26 de janeiro de 1226, em sua cidade natal, com 72 anos.

ro.Bronkhorst, Steenderen, Gelderland, Neth: Bronkhorst, Steenderen, Gelderland, Ţările de Jos

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Willem I van Bronchorst родился в 1154 г. в местности бронхаурст, стиндирен, джелдирланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Gijsbert I van Bronckhorst было 62 года, а матери, которую звали Heilwige van Roode, 37. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Gertrud von Ahaus. Он умер 26 января 1226 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 72 лет.

sm.1190-1226: mai le 1190 e o'o I le 1226

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Willem I van Bronchorst nació en 1154, en Bronkhorst, Steenderen, Gelderland, Países Bajos, su papá, Gijsbert I van Bronckhorst, tenía 62 años y su mamá, Heilwige van Roode, tenía 37 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Gertrud von Ahaus. Murió el 26 de enero de 1226, en su pueblo, a la edad de 72 años.
